title: Maintenance Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Maintenance Department.
Learn different types of industrial maintenance.
Understand the use of SAP PM in maintenance activities.

introduction.title: What is the Maintenance Department?
introduction.content: The Maintenance Department is responsible for ensuring that all plant machinery, equipment and utilities remain in safe and efficient working condition. The department performs inspections, preventive maintenance, repairs and equipment improvements to minimize downtime and maximize production efficiency.

business_purpose.title: Why is Maintenance Important?
business_purpose.content: Reliable equipment is essential for uninterrupted production. The Maintenance Department helps prevent unexpected machine failures, improves equipment life, reduces repair costs and ensures safe working conditions throughout the plant.

types_of_maintenance.description: Scheduled maintenance performed before equipment failure to prevent breakdowns.
Uses sensor data and condition monitoring to predict failures before they occur.
Repairs equipment after a fault is identified.
Emergency repair performed after equipment has failed.
Maintenance performed based on the actual condition of equipment.

key_responsibilities.description: Inspect equipment regularly for wear, damage and abnormalities.
Repair faulty machinery quickly and safely.
Coordinate spare part availability with Stores.
Ensure maintenance activities follow all safety procedures.

maintenance_workflow: Record maintenance details in SAP PM.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Plans maintenance activities without affecting production schedules.
Obtains required spare parts and consumables.
Procures replacement parts and maintenance materials.
Ensures repaired equipment maintains product quality.
Implements Lockout/Tagout and other safe maintenance practices.

business_example.title: Maintenance at JSL
business_example.content: During stainless steel production, vibration sensors detect abnormal bearing movement in a rolling mill motor. The Maintenance Department receives the alert through the monitoring system, schedules maintenance during planned downtime, replaces the damaged bearing and updates the maintenance history in SAP PM. This prevents an unexpected breakdown and avoids production losses.

best_practices: Analyze root causes of repeated failures.
important_notes: Preventive maintenance is more cost-effective than breakdown maintenance.
Proper documentation helps improve future maintenance planning.
Maintenance activities should always prioritize employee safety.
Reliable equipment contributes directly to production efficiency.
